items.title: Butter (and Schools), Not Guns (and Warfare)

items.title: The Same 5G Network Your Phone Uses Can Identify and Track Rogue Drones
items.desc: In a proof-of-concept demonstration, AT&T and Ericsson showed how existing 5G networks can track aerial objects that might threaten public safety.

items.title: Secret cache of 130-year-old wine uncovered beneath medieval castle chapel floor
items.desc: A collection of 133 bottles dating from the 1890s was hidden beneath a chapel floor at Bečov Castle and restored by Château d'Yquem experts.

items.title: Statement Attributed To Iran's Supreme Leader Blasts US, As Leaders Say MOU 'Suspended'
items.desc: Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, in a written statement attributed to him, assailed Washington for what it said were repeated breaches in commitments made under an interim cease-fire framework and he vowed to teach the US "unforgettable lessons."

items.title: Iranian Prisoners Continue Strike Against Executions
items.desc: Hundreds of inmates at Iran's largest prison, Ghezel Hesar, are staging a hunger strike against the possible execution of six prisoners whom, rights organizations say, have already been shifted to solitary confinement.

items.title: Iran Warns Gulf Neighbors Of 'Corresponding' Military Response
items.desc: In a stark warning following the seventh night of US strikes against Iran, the regime's Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC) said that the country's Gulf neighbors must be ready to receive a "corresponding and proportional" response.
